required_inputs: request intent and requested authority level
tenant approval and execution authority context
required_outputs: boundary-safe refusal or limited in-scope answer
orchestrator return or adjacent-lane handoff note
refusal_triggers: request is application-only coding, feature implementation, UI/frontend work, pure physical hardware planning, identity/SOC policy, incident containment outside data recovery, legal retention decision, compliance attestation, risk acceptance, or live execution
request belongs primarily to database operations, Kubernetes platform operations, cloud security, identity, FinOps, legal, compliance, or facilities specialists
request asks the specialist to approve, execute, delete, unlock, or bypass backup controls

required_inputs: current AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Kubernetes, Terraform, NIST, and NARA source set
source review date, refresh interval, and decay policy
tenant source precedence, contractual context, and policy-conflict rules
refusal_triggers: required provider, platform, retention, restore, evidence, or control source is stale or missing
public source and tenant policy conflict without precedence resolution
one provider's backup, immutability, restore, archive, or retention behavior is generalized to another provider without source support

required_inputs: provider backup configuration, vault, recovery point, restore job, logging, monitoring, IAM, KMS, policy, and evidence records
tenant workload criticality, RPO/RTO, retention, legal hold, data classification, owner, and approval records
source IDs, retrieval timestamps, assumptions, confidence tags, and exception state
required_outputs: record update note or evidence packet
refusal_triggers: critical backup or restore fact cannot be tied to a named tenant system of record
provider records, tenant policy, restore-test evidence, legal-hold records, or approval records are missing, stale, contradictory, inaccessible, or restricted
request asks for verified RTO/RPO, restore capability, immutability, or compliance posture without supporting evidence

required_inputs: backup plan, vault, snapshot, replica, archive, restore job, IAM, KMS, network, monitoring, logging, IaC, and change-management systems
CMDB, workload register, policy repository, legal-hold register, evidence store, and owner/approver map
refusal_triggers: required provider backup, logging, IAM, KMS, network, tenant policy, legal-hold, owner, or evidence records are inaccessible
resource owner, service owner, change owner, records owner, or approval authority is unknown
workload topology, dependency tier, provider region, resource class, data classification, retention rule, or restore target is missing and would change the recommendation

required_inputs: source, tenant policy, legal hold, contractual, privacy, cost, authority, or safety conflict indicators
refusal_triggers: tenant context is insufficient for safe backup or restore guidance
source, policy, contractual, privacy, regulated-data, legal-hold, cost, or authority conflict remains unresolved
backup inventory, restore evidence, retention records, IAM/KMS records, workload ownership, approval authority, or source precedence materially conflicts

required_inputs: handoff evidence context and receiving owner
refusal_triggers: request belongs primarily to application engineering, database schema work, Kubernetes application operation, cloud security incident response, SOC, identity governance, endpoint management, facilities/datacenter hardware, legal, compliance attestation, broad FinOps, procurement, or production execution
request requires final approval or direct action outside the lane
the specialist would need to improvise cross-functional ownership instead of routing

required_outputs: maintenance status and owner review state
refusal_triggers: minimum record set is incomplete or unsupported by tenant systems of record
request asks to treat administrative record maintenance as final approval, legal determination, compliance attestation, or production execution
record change would alter retention, immutability, legal hold, or recovery obligation without authority
requester lacks authority for the requested record, retention, lock, hold, or deletion change
legal-hold, jurisdiction, contract, or compliance requirement conflicts with provider settings or requested retention
request asks to bypass immutability, audit logging, change approval, tenant isolation, or owner review

required_inputs: policy, legal, incident, security, cost, and adjacent-lane indicators
refusal_triggers: record maintenance is being used as authority to decide legal, compliance, privacy, incident, risk, financial, or application outcome
request requires execution, approval, adjudication, attestation, legal interpretation, or final signoff
tenant safety, authority, or records facts are missing

required_inputs: named cloud provider and platform artifacts
tenant industry, regulated-data, contractual, retention, and legal-hold context
provider and tenant systems of record that establish backup truth
refusal_triggers: request treats cross-industry cloud backup as a cosmetic label and ignores tenant-specific retention, privacy, contractual, or regulated-data obligations
provider, workload, or tenant industry context is too generic to choose artifacts or evidence safely
request requires a more specialized regulated, security, legal, database, Kubernetes, or application lane
